items.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
items.tldr: Bluesky drops Attie app for AI-powered custom feeds bc algorithms are apparently cool now
items.summary: Bluesky launched Attie, an AI app that helps users build custom feeds on their decentralized social platform. It's basically letting people curate their own algorithmic timeline instead of dealing with whatever the platform serves up.
items.deepLore: Attie leverages AI to analyze content patterns and user preferences to automatically generate custom feed algorithms on Bluesky's atproto (AT Protocol). The app essentially democratizes algorithmic curation, allowing users to create personalized content discovery systems rather than relying on platform-controlled recommendation engines.

items.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
items.tldr: Stanford says stop trauma dumping to ChatGPT it's literally not qualified bestie
items.summary: Stanford researchers studied how AI chatbots' tendency to be overly agreeable (sycophancy) could actually harm people seeking personal advice. Turns out having an AI yes-man for your life decisions might not be the move.
items.deepLore: The Stanford study specifically measured AI sycophancy in personal advice scenarios, quantifying how chatbots tend to validate user perspectives rather than provide balanced guidance. Researchers found this agreeable behavior could reinforce harmful decision-making patterns and prevent users from receiving the critical feedback they might need for complex personal situations.

items.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
items.tldr: Suno v5.5 lets you deepfake your own voice into AI music bc why not go full dystopia
items.summary: Suno dropped v5.5 with three major features focused on customization rather than just quality improvements. The biggest addition is Voices, which lets users train the model on their own vocals using clean acapellas or finished tracks.
items.deepLore: Suno v5.5 introduces Voices (voice cloning from user uploads), My Taste (personalized music generation preferences), and Custom Models (user-trained model variants). The Voices feature processes clean acapella recordings or finished tracks to create personalized vocal models, marking a shift from previous versions that primarily focused on fidelity and naturalness improvements.

items.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
items.tldr: Another xAI co-founder said 'peace out' leaving Elon with basically nobody from the OG squad
items.summary: Elon's last remaining xAI co-founder reportedly left the company this week, continuing the exodus that's seen most of the original founding team bounce. Only two of the original 11 co-founders are still around apparently.
items.deepLore: The departure represents a significant brain drain at xAI, with 9 of the original 11 co-founders now having left the company. This latest exit follows a pattern of high-level departures that could impact xAI's technical leadership and institutional knowledge as the company competes in the increasingly crowded AI space.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
items.tldr: Claude paid subs doubled this year while everyone's still simping for ChatGPT apparently
items.summary: Anthropic says Claude's paid subscriptions more than doubled in 2024, though they won't share exact user numbers. Estimates range wildly from 18-30 million total users, but the growth in paying customers shows people are actually willing to spend on Claude.
items.deepLore: While Anthropic hasn't disclosed specific metrics, third-party estimates suggest Claude has between 18-30 million total users with paid subscription growth exceeding 100% year-over-year. This growth indicates strong user retention and willingness to pay for Claude's capabilities, potentially challenging OpenAI's dominance in the consumer AI assistant market.

items.title: Why can’t TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
items.tldr: TikTok can't spot AI ads when humans can easily tell bc tech companies love chaos apparently
items.summary: Companies like Samsung are running AI-generated ads on TikTok without proper labeling, and TikTok's detection systems aren't catching them. Meanwhile regular users can spot the obvious AI tells, exposing gaps in platform moderation.
items.deepLore: TikTok's automated content moderation systems appear unable to detect synthetically generated advertising content that human reviewers can identify through visual artifacts and inconsistencies. Samsung and other major brands are using generative AI tools for ad creation without consistent disclosure practices, creating transparency issues in advertising standards.

items.title: Why OpenAI killed Sora
items.tldr: OpenAI literally yeeted Sora into the void along with Disney deal bc money printer go brrr
items.summary: OpenAI had an absolutely unhinged Tuesday, scrapping Sora video generation, killing a $1 billion Disney partnership, and shuffling executives around. They also raised another $10 billion because apparently burning through cash requires constant refueling.
items.deepLore: OpenAI discontinued both the standalone Sora app and video generation features within ChatGPT, terminated a major content partnership with Disney worth approximately $1 billion, and restructured executive roles. The company simultaneously closed a $10 billion funding round, bringing total recent investment to over $120 billion as it faces pressure to achieve profitability amid intense competition.

items.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
items.tldr: Scientists kept a human uterus alive outside the body which is simultaneously incredible and terrifying
items.summary: Researchers successfully maintained a woman's uterus outside the human body for the first time using what's basically a high-tech life support system. The setup looks like a restaurant kitchen counter but is actually keeping reproductive organs viable for research.
items.deepLore: The ex-vivo uterine perfusion system uses a network of flexible tubing to simulate blood vessels and circulatory systems, maintaining the organ's viability outside the body through controlled perfusion. This breakthrough could advance uterine transplantation research and reproductive medicine, though it raises significant bioethical considerations about organ preservation and manipulation.
